package.name: msig
package.description: Browser-first JavaScript SDK for mutational signature analysis with mSigPortal data, plotting, and researcher workflow helpers.

namespaces.name: mSigPortal
namespaces.label: mSigPortal
namespaces.summary: mSigPortal project, sample, signature, exposure, matrix, and plot helpers.

functions.name: extractMutationalSpectra
functions.description: Extracts the mutational spectra out of the mSigPortal API call.

params.name: data
params.description: An array of objects containing the data from the mSigPortal API call.

params.name: groupName
params.description: The name of the group to extract the mutational spectra from.

returns.description: An object containing the mutational spectra data grouped by the specified group name.

functions.name: formatMutationalSpectraData
functions.description: Converts the mutational spectra data to a format that can be used to create a Plotly chart.

params.name: mutationalSpectrum
params.description: An object containing the mutational spectra data.

returns.description: The data in a format that can be used to create a Plotly chart. The data is an array of objects. Each object has a name, x, y, and type property. The name property is the name of the mutation type. The x property is an array of the mutation names. The y property is an array of the mutation frequencies. The type property is the type of substitution that takes place.

functions.name: getMutationalSignatureActivityData
functions.description: Retrieves mutational signature landscape data from the mutational-signatures API.

params.name: study
params.description: The name of the study. Default value is "PCAWG".

params.name: genomeDataType
params.description: The type of genome data. Default value is "WGS".

params.name: cancerType
params.description: The name of the cancer type. Default value is an empty string.

params.name: signatureSetName
params.description: The name of the signature set. Default value is "COSMIC_v3_Signatures_GRCh37_SBS96".

params.name: numberOfResults
params.description: The maximum number of results to be returned. Default value is 10.

returns.description: A Promise that resolves to the JSON data of the mutational signature landscape.

functions.name: getMutationalSignatureActivityOptions
functions.description: Retrieves a list of mutational signature activity options from the mutational signatures API.

params.name: study
params.description: The name of the study to retrieve mutational signature activity options for. Defaults to "PCAWG".

params.name: genomeDataType
params.description: The genome data type to retrieve mutational signature activity options for. Defaults to "WGS".

params.name: numberOfResults
params.description: The number of results to retrieve. Defaults to 10.

returns.description: A promise that resolves with an array of mutational signature activity options.

functions.name: getMutationalSignatureAssociationData
functions.description: Retrieves mutational signature association data from a specified cancer study using the provided parameters.

params.name: study
params.description: The name of the cancer study. Default is "PCAWG".

params.name: genomeDataType
params.description: The type of genome data used. Default is "WGS".

params.name: cancerType
params.description: The type of cancer. Default is "Biliary-AdenoCA".

params.name: numberOfResults
params.description: The maximum number of results to return. Default is 10.

returns.description: A Promise that resolves to the JSON response containing the mutational signature association data.

functions.name: getMutationalSignatureAssociationOptions
functions.description: Fetches the mutational signature association options from the API endpoint

params.name: study
params.description: The name of the study. Defaults to "PCAWG".

params.name: genomeDataType
params.description: The type of genome data. Defaults to "WGS".

params.name: numberOfResults
params.description: The number of results to return. Defaults to 10.

returns.description: A Promise that resolves to an array of mutational signature association options.

functions.name: getMutationalSignatureEtiologyData
functions.description: Retrieves mutational signature etiology data from the Cancer Genomics Research Laboratory (CGR) website.

params.name: genomeDataType
params.description: The genome data type. Default is "WGS".

params.name: cancerType
params.description: The cancer type. Default is an empty string.

params.name: numberOfResults
params.description: The number of results to return. Default is 10.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the mutational signature etiology data in JSON format.

functions.name: getMutationalSignatureEtiologyOptions
functions.description: Retrieves the etiology options for a given mutational signature from the Cancer.gov Mutational Signatures API.

params.name: study
params.description: The name of the study to retrieve etiology options for. Defaults to "PCAWG".

params.name: genomeDataType
params.description: The type of genome data to retrieve etiology options for. Defaults to "WGS".

params.name: signatureName
params.description: The name of the mutational signature to retrieve etiology options for. Defaults to "SBS3".

params.name: cancerType
params.description: The cancer type to retrieve etiology options for. Defaults to an empty string.

params.name: numberOfResults
params.description: The maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 10.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an object representing the etiology options for the specified mutational signature. The object will have the following properties: etiology: An array of strings representing the possible etiologies for the mutational signature. etiology_display: An array of strings representing the display names for the possible etiologies. signatureName: The name of the mutational signature. study: The name of the study. genome_data_type: The type of genome data. cancer_type: The cancer type.

functions.name: getMutationalSignatureLandscapeData
functions.description: Retrieves mutational signature landscape data from an API endpoint.

params.name: study
params.description: The study to retrieve data from.

params.name: genomeDataType
params.description: The type of genomic data used in the study.

params.name: cancerType
params.description: The type of cancer to retrieve data for.

params.name: signatureSetName
params.description: The name of the mutational signature set to retrieve.

params.name: numberOfResults
params.description: The number of results to retrieve.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an object containing the mutational signature landscape data.

functions.name: getMutationalSignaturesData

params.name: genomeDataType
params.description: The type of genome data to use. Defaults to "WGS".

params.name: signatureSetName
params.description: The name of the signature set to use. Defaults to "COSMIC_v3_Signatures_GRCh37_SBS96".

params.name: mutationType
params.description: The type of mutation to analyze. Defaults to "SBS".

params.name: matrix
params.description: The size of the mutational signature matrix. Defaults to 96.

params.name: numberofResults
params.description: The number of results to retrieve. Defaults to 10.

returns.description: A Promise that resolves to the unformatted mutational signatures data as JSON.

examples:
const mutationalSignatures = await getMutationalSignaturesData("WGS", "COSMIC_v3_Signatures_GRCh37_SBS96", "SBS", 96, 10);
console.log(mutationalSignatures);

functions.name: getMutationalSignaturesOptions
functions.description: Retrieves the mutational signature options from the specified API endpoint.

params.name: genomeDataType
params.description: The genome data type to use. Defaults to "WGS".

params.name: mutationType
params.description: The mutation type to use. Defaults to "SBS".

returns.description: A Promise that resolves to the mutational signature options in JSON format.

examples:
const mutationalSignatures = await getMutationalSignaturesOptions("WGS", "SBS");
console.log(mutationalSignatures);

functions.name: getMutationalSignaturesSummary
functions.description: Returns a summary of mutational signatures based on the provided signature set name and number of results.

params.name: numberofResults
params.description: The number of results to retrieve. Defaults to 10 if not provided.

params.name: signatureSetName
params.description: The name of the signature set to retrieve. Defaults to "COSMIC_v3.3_Signatures" if not provided.

returns.description: A Promise that resolves to an object representing the mutational signature summary.

examples:
const summary = await getMutationalSignaturesSummary(20, "COSMIC_v3.3_Signatures");
console.log(summary);

functions.name: getMutationalSpectrumData
functions.description: Fetches mutational spectrum data from the Cancer Genomics Data Server API and returns it in a formatted way.

params.name: samples
params.description: The sample identifier(s) to query data for.

params.name: matrixSize
params.description: The size of the mutational spectrum matrix.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the formatted mutational spectrum data.

functions.name: getMutationalSpectrumOptions
functions.description: Retrieves mutational spectrum options from the mutational signatures API.

params.name: study
params.description: The name of the study to retrieve options for. Defaults to "PCAWG".

params.name: genomeDataType
params.description: The genome data type to retrieve options for. Defaults to "WGS".

params.name: cancerType
params.description: The cancer type to retrieve options for. Defaults to "Lung-AdenoCA".

params.name: numberOfResults
params.description: The number of results to retrieve. Defaults to 10.

returns.description: A Promise that resolves to the JSON response from the mutational signatures API.

functions.name: getMutationalSpectrumSummary
functions.description: Fetches the mutational spectrum summary from the mutational signatures API based on the given parameters.

params.name: study
params.description: The name of the cancer genome study. Default is "PCAWG".

params.name: genomeDataType
params.description: The type of genomic data used. Default is "WGS".

params.name: cancerType
params.description: The type of cancer. Default is "Lung-AdenoCA".

params.name: numberOfResults
params.description: The number of results to be returned. Default is 10.

returns.description: A Promise that resolves to a JSON object representing the mutational spectrum summary.

functions.name: MsAssociation
functions.description: Calculates the association between two mutational signatures across a set of samples. It computes the linear regression, Pearson correlation, and Spearman correlation between the log-transformed exposures of the two signatures. The results are used to generate a scatter plot with marginal histograms, visualizing the relationship between the signatures.

params.name: data
params.description: An array of objects representing the exposure data for a set of samples. Each object in the array should have the following properties:sample: A string representing the sample ID.signatureName: A string representing the name of the mutational signature.exposure: A numeric value representing the exposure of the signature in the sample. This value can theoretically range from 0 to infinity, although in practice, values are often normalized.

params.name: signatureName1
params.description: The name of the first mutational signature. This should match the signatureName values in the data array.

params.name: signatureName2
params.description: The name of the second mutational signature. This should also match the signatureName values in the data array. It can be the same as signatureName1.

params.name: both
params.description: A boolean flag indicating whether to filter the data to include only samples where both signatures have non-zero exposure.true: Only samples with non-zero exposure to both signatures are included in the analysis. If signatureName1 and signatureName2 are the same, then no filtering occurs.false: All samples are included in the analysis, regardless of whether they have non-zero exposure to both signatures.

returns.description: Returns an object containing the traces and layout for a Plotly plot.traces: An array of trace objects to be used in a Plotly plot. This includes the main scatter plot trace, the linear regression line trace, and two marginal histogram traces.layout: An object containing the layout configuration for a Plotly plot, including title, axis labels, annotations, and other visual properties.

functions.name: MSPrevalence
functions.description: Calculates and visualizes the prevalence of mutational signatures across a set of samples, grouped by cancer type. The function generates two plots: a pie chart showing the overall prevalence of each signature based on total mutations (exposure) and a bar chart displaying the frequency of each signature across samples, considering a minimum exposure threshold.

params.name: data
params.description: An object representing the mutational signature prevalence data. The data object is expected to be an array of objects with the following structure: [{ signatureName: "SBS1", sample: "sample1", exposure: 10, burden: 5 }, { signatureName: "SBS5", sample: "sample1", exposure: 20, burden: 5 }, ... ] Where signatureName is the name of a mutational signature (string), sample is a sample identifier (string), exposure is a non-negative number representing the exposure of that signature in the sample (can be 0), and burden is a numeric value representing the mutational burden for the sample (must be a number).

params.name: minimum
params.description: The minimum exposure value for a signature in a sample to be considered prevalent in that sample. Samples with exposure below this threshold are not counted in the frequency calculation for the bar chart. If minimum is null or undefined, it defaults to 100.null or undefined: Sets the minimum exposure to 100.Any positive number: Sets the minimum exposure to that number.

returns.description: Returns an object containing the traces and layout for a Plotly plot.traces: An array of trace objects. If the maximum frequency of signatures (considering the minimum threshold) is less than 1%, the array contains only a single pie chart trace. Otherwise, it contains a pie chart trace followed by multiple bar chart traces (one for each signature).layout: An object defining the layout of the plot, including title annotations, axis settings, and overall appearance. It includes conditional logic to handle cases where no signature has a frequency greater than 1%.

functions.name: plotCosineSimilarityHeatMap
functions.description: Generates a cosine similarity heatmap based on mutational spectra data. This function processes grouped mutational data to compute cosine similarities, optionally performs double clustering to reorder the data, and then visualizes the similarities using a Plotly heatmap. It also supports displaying a table representation of the cosine similarity matrix alongside the heatmap.

params.name: groupedData
params.description: The input data object where keys represent sample names and values are objects representing mutational spectra. The mutational spectra should be represented as key-value pairs where keys are mutation types and values are counts or frequencies. Example:{ 'Sample1': {'C>A': 10, 'C>G': 15, 'C>T': 20, ...}, 'Sample2': {'C>A': 5, 'C>G': 8, 'C>T': 12, ...}, ... }The range of keys (mutation types) should be consistent across all samples. The values (counts or frequencies) can be integers or floats, and their range can vary based on the underlying data, but typically they are non-negative.

params.name: studyName
params.description: The name of the study. This is used in the title of the heatmap. Common values include study identifiers like "PCAWG", "TCGA", or specific project names. The parameter should be a string and can technically accept any string value, but it is intended to represent the name of a study or dataset.

params.name: genomeDataType
params.description: The type of genomic data. This is also used in the title of the heatmap. Expected values typically include abbreviations for common genomic data types such as "WGS" (Whole Genome Sequencing), "WES" (Whole Exome Sequencing), "RNA-Seq", etc. Similar to studyName, any string is technically accepted, but the intended use is to describe the data type.

params.name: cancerType
params.description: The type of cancer. This is included in the title of the heatmap. Common values are standard cancer type abbreviations or names, like "Lung-AdenoCA" (Lung Adenocarcinoma), "BRCA" (Breast Invasive Carcinoma), etc. Any string value is accepted, but it should represent a specific cancer type.

params.name: divID
params.description: The target element or target element ID where the heatmap will be rendered. If an element with this ID does not exist, a detached element will be created and returned. Observable notebooks can render that returned element in the current cell.

params.name: conductDoubleClustering
params.description: A flag indicating whether to perform double clustering (hierarchical clustering on both rows and columns) on the cosine similarity matrix. If true, the rows and columns of the heatmap will be reordered based on the clustering. If false, the order of samples in groupedData will be maintained. Boolean values true or false are expected.

params.name: colorscale
params.description: The Plotly colorscale to use for the heatmap. This can be any valid Plotly colorscale name (e.g., "Viridis", "Greys", "YlGnBu", "RdBu"). Plotly provides a wide range of predefined colorscales. The chosen colorscale will affect the visual representation of the similarity values. Any string is accepted but it should correspond to a valid Plotly colorscale for optimal results.

params.name: showTable
params.description: A flag indicating whether to display a table representation of the cosine similarity matrix alongside the heatmap. If true, a table will be rendered next to the heatmap. If false, only the heatmap will be displayed. Boolean values true or false are expected.

functions.name: plotDatasetMutationalSignaturesExposure
functions.description: Generates a heatmap visualizing the exposure of multiple samples to a set of mutational signatures within a dataset. The function provides options for displaying relative or absolute exposure values and for performing double hierarchical clustering to reorder the rows and columns of the heatmap. It also allows customization of the color scale used to represent exposure values.

params.name: exposureData
params.description: An object containing the exposure data for a set of samples. The structure of exposureData is expected to be: { sampleId1: { signatureName1: exposureValue1, signatureName2: exposureValue2, ..., rnorm: number }, sampleId2: { signatureName1: exposureValue3, signatureName2: exposureValue4, ..., rnorm: number }, ... } The outer keys (e.g., sampleId1, sampleId2) are sample identifiers (strings). The inner objects (e.g., { signatureName1: exposureValue1, ... }) represent the exposure values for a given sample. signatureName keys are strings representing the names of mutational signatures (e.g., "SBS1", "SBS5"), and exposureValue are non-negative numbers representing the contribution of that signature to the sample. rnorm is a number that will be removed from the data before plotting.

params.name: divID
params.description: The ID of the HTML div element where the heatmap will be rendered.

params.name: relative
params.description: A boolean indicating whether to display relative or absolute exposure values.true: The exposure values for each sample are normalized to sum to 1, representing the relative contribution of each signature.false: The raw exposure values are displayed.

params.name: datasetName
params.description: The name of the dataset being visualized. This is used as part of the plot title. Examples include:"PCAWG""TCGA"Any other string that appropriately identifies the dataset.

params.name: doubleCluster
params.description: A boolean indicating whether to perform double hierarchical clustering on the exposure data.true: The rows and columns of the heatmap are reordered based on the results of double clustering, which groups similar samples and signatures together.false: The rows and columns are displayed in the order they appear in the input exposureData.

params.name: colorscale
params.description: The color scale to use for the heatmap. Possible values are:"Custom": A predefined custom color scale designed for visualizing exposure data.Any valid Plotly color scale name (e.g., "Viridis", "Blues", "Hot", etc.).An array of arrays defining a custom color scale, where each inner array specifies a color stop with a value between 0 and 1 and a corresponding RGB color string (e.g., [["0.0", "rgb(49,54,149)"], ["1.0", "rgb(165,0,38)"]]).

returns.description: Returns the data object used by Plotly to generate the heatmap. This object contains the z values (exposure values), x values (signature names), y values (sample names), and other settings for the heatmap, including the color scale. The structure is: { z: [[exposureValue1, exposureValue2, ...], [exposureValue3, exposureValue4, ...], ...], x: [signatureName1, signatureName2, ...], y: [sampleId1, sampleId2, ...], type: "heatmap", colorscale: colorscale }

functions.name: plotForceDirectedTree
functions.description: This function generates and displays a force-directed tree representing the relationships between patients in a study based on their mutational spectra. It calculates the cosine similarity between the mutational spectra of patients, performs hierarchical clustering based on these similarities, and then visualizes the resulting clusters as a force-directed tree.

params.name: groupedData
params.description: An object where keys represent sample IDs and values are objects containing mutational spectra data. The structure of groupedData is expected to be: { sampleId1: { mutationType1: count1, mutationType2: count2, ... }, sampleId2: { mutationType1: count3, mutationType2: count4, ... }, ... } The inner objects (e.g., { mutationType1: count1, ... }) represent the mutational spectrum for a given sample. mutationType keys can be any string representing a type of mutation (e.g., "C>A", "T>G"), and count values are non-negative integers representing the number of times that mutation type is observed in the sample.

params.name: studyName
params.description: The name of the study. This is used for labeling purposes in the visualization. Common values include, but are not limited to:"PCAWG""TCGA"Any other string representing a specific study.

params.name: genomeDataType
params.description: The type of genome data used. This is also used for labeling purposes. Possible values include:"WGS": Whole Genome Sequencing"WES": Whole Exome Sequencing"RNA-Seq": RNA Sequencing

params.name: cancerType
params.description: The type of cancer being studied. This is used for labeling in the visualization. Examples include:"Lung-AdenoCA": Lung Adenocarcinoma"Breast-AdenoCA": Breast AdenocarcinomaAny valid cancer type identifier.

params.name: divID
params.description: The ID of the HTML div element where the force-directed tree will be rendered.

returns.description: Returns the formatted hierarchical clusters used to generate the force-directed tree. The structure of this object is compatible with the AM5 charting library and represents the hierarchical relationships between samples based on their mutational spectra. The format is a nested object where each level represents a node in the tree. Each node can have properties such as name, value, children (an array of child nodes), and potentially others added during formatting.

functions.name: plotMSPrevalenceData
functions.description: This function is a wrapper around the plotMSPrevalence function. It takes the output of plotMSPrevalence and uses it to generate a Plotly plot, which is then displayed in a specified div. The plot visualizes the prevalence of mutational signatures.

params.name: divID
params.description: The ID of the div element where the plot will be rendered.

params.name: data
params.description: An object representing the mutational signature prevalence data. The data object is expected to be an array of objects with the following structure: [{ signatureName: "SBS1", sample: "sample1", exposure: 10, burden: 5 }, { signatureName: "SBS5", sample: "sample1", exposure: 20, burden: 5 }, ... ] Where signatureName is the name of a mutational signature (string), sample is a sample identifier (string), exposure is a non-negative number representing the exposure of that signature in the sample, and burden is a numeric value representing the mutational burden for the sample.

returns.description: This function does not return a value. It directly renders the plot in the specified divID.

functions.name: plotPatientMutationalSignaturesExposure
functions.description: Generates a pie chart visualizing the exposure of a single sample to a set of mutational signatures. The function takes exposure data, which includes the relative contribution of each signature to the sample's mutational profile, and displays it in a pie chart format.

params.name: exposureData
params.description: An object containing exposure data for either a set of samples or a single sample. The structure of exposureData can be: { sampleId1: { signatureName1: exposureValue1, signatureName2: exposureValue2, ... }, sampleId2: { signatureName1: exposureValue3, signatureName2: exposureValue4, ... }, ... } or a single sample object: { signatureName1: exposureValue1, signatureName2: exposureValue2, ... }. The outer keys (e.g., sampleId1, sampleId2) are sample identifiers (strings). The inner objects (e.g., { signatureName1: exposureValue1, ... }) represent the exposure values for a given sample. signatureName keys are strings representing the names of mutational signatures (e.g., "SBS1", "SBS5"), and exposureValue are non-negative numbers representing the contribution of that signature to the sample. These values typically sum to 1 for each sample. The exposureData object can contain multiple samples, but only the data for the specified sample will be used for plotting. Each sample may also have an optional rnorm property which is a number.

params.name: divID
params.description: The ID of the HTML div element where the pie chart will be rendered.

params.name: sample
params.description: The ID of the sample for which to plot the mutational signature exposure. This should be one of the keys in the exposureData object (e.g., "sampleId1", "sampleId2").

returns.description: Returns the data object used by Plotly to generate the pie chart. This object contains the labels (signature names), values (exposure values), and other settings for the pie chart. The format is: { labels: [signatureName1, signatureName2, ...], values: [exposureValue1, exposureValue2, ...], name: "sample exposure values", textposition: "inside", hole: 0.4, hoverinfo: "name + value", type: "pie" }

functions.name: plotPatientMutationalSpectrum
functions.description: Renders a plot of mutational profiles in a given div element ID.

params.name: mutationalSpectra
params.description: An object containing the mutational spectra data for one or more patients.

params.name: matrixSize
params.description: The size of the plot matrix. Defaults to 96.

params.name: divID
params.description: The ID of the div element to render the plot in. Defaults to 'mutationalSpectrumMatrix'.

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the plot has been rendered.

functions.name: plotPatientMutationalSpectrumuserData
functions.description: Plots the mutational spectrum for the given parameters.

params.name: mutationalSpectra
params.description: An object containing the mutational spectra data.

params.name: matrixSize
params.description: The size of the matrix to be plotted.

params.name: divID
params.description: The ID of the div element where the plot will be displayed.

functions.name: plotProfilerSummary
functions.description: Generates a mutational spectrum summary plot and displays it in a given HTML div element.

params.name: studyName
params.description: The name of the cancer genomics study to use. Default is "PCAWG".

params.name: genomeDataType
params.description: The type of genomic data to use. Default is "WGS".

params.name: cancerTypeOrGroup
params.description: The cancer type or group to display. Default is "Lung-AdenoCA".

params.name: numberOfResults
params.description: The maximum number of results to display. Default is 50.

params.name: divID
params.description: The ID of the HTML div element where the plot will be displayed. Default is "mutationalSpectrumSummary".

returns.description: A Promise that resolves when the plot is displayed or rejects if there is an error.

functions.name: plotProjectMutationalBurdenByCancerType
functions.description: Plots the mutational burden by cancer type for a given project.

params.name: project
params.description: An object containing mutational data for different cancer types.

params.name: divID
params.description: The ID of the div where the plot should be displayed.

returns.description: A Promise that resolves when the plot is displayed.

examples:
// Example usage:
plotProjectMutationalBurdenByCancerType(projectData, "plotDiv");

functions.name: plotSignatureActivityDataBy
functions.description: Generates a box plot of signature activity data, grouped by a specified attribute. The function takes a dataset and groups it by the provided attribute (e.g., "signatureName", "study", "cancerType"). For each group, it creates a box trace where the y-values represent the log10 of the exposure values and the x-values are set to the group name. The box plot displays the distribution of exposure values for each group, with the option to show all individual data points (jittered for better visibility). Hovering over the data points reveals the sample name and the log10 of the exposure value. The plot also indicates the fraction of samples within each group that have non-zero exposure.

params.name: divID
params.description: The ID of the div element where the plot will be rendered.

params.name: data
params.description: An array of objects representing the signature activity data. Each object must have at least the following properties:exposure: A numeric value representing the exposure of a signature. It can be any positive number or zero.sample: A string representing the sample ID.The data array must also contain a property matching the name specified by the group parameter (e.g., "signatureName", "study", "cancerType").

params.name: group
params.description: The attribute to group the data by. Possible values are any property name present in the data objects, including, but not limited to:"signatureName": Groups the data by signature names."study": Groups the data by study IDs."cancerType": Groups the data by cancer types."sample": Groups the data by sample IDs (Note: this might not result in a meaningful box plot).Any other custom property that exists in the data objects. The default value is "signatureName".

returns.description: This function does not return a value. It directly renders the plot in the specified divID.

functions.name: plotSignatureAssociations
functions.description: This function generates and plots a scatter plot with marginal histograms, along with statistical analysis, to visualize the association between two mutational signatures. It calculates and displays the linear regression line, Pearson correlation, and Spearman correlation, providing insights into the relationship between the exposures of two signatures in a set of samples.

params.name: divID
params.description: The ID of the HTML div element where the plot will be rendered.

params.name: data
params.description: An array of objects representing the exposure data for a set of samples. Each object in the array should have the following properties:sample: A string representing the sample ID.signatureName: A string representing the name of the mutational signature.exposure: A numeric value representing the exposure of the signature in the sample.

params.name: signature1
params.description: The name of the first mutational signature. This should match the signatureName values in the data array. The values can be any valid signature name present in the dataset, for example, "SBS1", "SBS5", "DBS1", "ID4".

params.name: signature2
params.description: The name of the second mutational signature. This should also match the signatureName values in the data array. Similar to signature1, the values can be any signature name present in the dataset and can also be the same as signature1 to assess the distribution of a single signature.

returns.description: This function does not return a value. It directly renders the plot in the specified divID.

functions.name: plotUMAPVisualization
functions.description: Generates a UMAP (Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection) visualization of mutational spectra data. UMAP is a dimensionality reduction technique used to project high-dimensional data into a lower-dimensional space (typically 2D or 3D) while preserving the global structure of the data. This function takes mutational spectra data, applies UMAP to reduce its dimensionality, and then creates either a 2D or 3D scatter plot to visualize the results. If nComponents is set to 3, it additionally generates a mesh3d trace to highlight the density of points in the 3D space.

params.name: data
params.description: An object representing the mutational spectra data. The structure of data is expected to be: { sampleId1: { mutationType1: count1, mutationType2: count2, ... }, sampleId2: { mutationType1: count3, mutationType2: count4, ... }, ... } The outer keys (e.g., sampleId1, sampleId2) are sample identifiers (strings). The inner objects (e.g., { mutationType1: count1, ... }) represent the mutational spectrum for a given sample. mutationType keys can be any string representing a type of mutation (e.g., "C>A", "T>G"), and count values are non-negative integers representing the number of times that mutation type is observed in the sample.

params.name: datasetName
params.description: The name of the dataset being visualized. This is used as part of the plot title. Examples include:"PCAWG""TCGA"Any other string that appropriately identifies the dataset.

params.name: divID
params.description: The ID of the HTML div element where the plot will be rendered.

params.name: nComponents
params.description: The number of dimensions to reduce the data to using UMAP. This determines whether a 2D or 3D plot is generated. Possible values are:2: Generates a 2D scatter plot.3: Generates a 3D scatter plot with an additional mesh3d trace. Any other positive integer is technically permissible but may not yield meaningful visualizations.

params.name: minDist
params.description: The effective minimum distance between embedded points in the UMAP projection. Smaller values result in a more clustered embedding, while larger values preserve more of the global structure. The valid range is between 0.0 and 1.0.

params.name: nNeighbors
params.description: The number of neighboring points to consider when constructing the UMAP. Larger values capture more global structure in the data, while smaller values preserve more local structure. Values should be positive integers, typically in the range of 2 to 100.

returns.description: Returns the trace object used by Plotly to generate the visualization. This object contains the data points, plot type, marker settings, and, in the case of a 3D plot, the mesh3d settings. The structure depends on the value of nComponents.

namespaces.name: userData
namespaces.label: User data
namespaces.summary: MAF, panel, and user-supplied spectrum conversion utilities.

functions.name: convertMatrix
functions.description: Converts input mutational data into a mutational spectrum matrix grouped by a specified field.This function processes raw mutational data, extracts trinucleotide contexts, and aggregates mutational spectra for each group. It supports TCGA and non-TCGA formats, allowing batch processing for large datasets. The resulting mutational spectrum matrix is formatted for downstream visualization and analysis.

params.name: data
params.description: Flat MAF-like rows or nested patient-level mutational data. Each row contains mutational details (e.g., chromosome, position, mutation type).

params.name: group_by
params.description: Field to group data by (e.g., "Center" or "sample_id"). This field should exist in the input data.

params.name: batch_size
params.description: Number of mutations to process in parallel batches. Adjust this for memory management.

params.name: genome
params.description: Reference genome build. Defaults to "hg19" unless specified in the data.

params.name: tcga
params.description: Flag indicating whether the input data is in TCGA format. If true, expects TCGA-specific fields.

properties.name: options.offline
properties.description: Use row-supplied or lookup-table contexts instead of the UCSC sequence API.

returns.description: A promise resolving to an object where each key is a group (e.g., "CNIC"), and each value is a mutational spectrum object. The mutational spectrum object contains trinucleotide contexts as keys (e.g., "A[C>A]A") and counts as values.

functions.name: convertMutationalSpectraIntoJSON
functions.description: Converts mutational spectra into JSON objects for downstream processing or storage.This function takes mutation annotation format (MAF) files, mutational spectra data, and sample names, and outputs JSON objects representing the spectra in a structured format.

params.name: MAFfiles
params.description: An array of arrays containing mutation annotation data for each sample. Each inner array represents a sample's mutation data, where each entry is an object with key-value pairs.

params.name: mutSpec
params.description: An object representing the mutational spectra. Keys are patient identifiers, and values are objects with mutation types as keys and counts as values.

params.name: sample_name
params.description: The key in MAFfiles to be used as the sample identifier.

params.name: dataType
params.description: The sequencing strategy, e.g., "WGS" (Whole Genome Sequencing) or "WES" (Whole Exome Sequencing).

returns.description: An array of arrays, where each inner array represents the JSON objects for a patient's mutational spectra.

functions.name: convertWGStoPanel
functions.description: Converts Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS) mutation data into panel data by downsampling based on a BED file.This function takes WGS mutation annotation files (MAFs) and a BED file defining panel regions, then filters the WGS data to include only mutations within the specified panel regions.

params.name: WgMAFs
params.description: An array of WGS mutation data, where each element is an array representing mutations for a single sample. Each mutation record should be an object with fields such as chromosome, start_position, etc.

params.name: panelDf
params.description: A BED file defining the regions of the panel or an array of arrays representing the panel regions. If a string is provided, it is treated as a file path to a BED file and read into memory.

returns.description: An array of downsampled MAFs, where each element corresponds to a sample from the input WGS data, filtered to include only mutations within the panel regions.

functions.name: createWGStoPanelValidationPairs
functions.description: Creates matched WGS and panel spectra pairs from a WGS spectra matrix and callable-opportunity mask.

params.name: panelCallableOpportunityMask
params.description: Shared or sample-specific callable opportunities by context.

properties.name: options.binaryMask
properties.description: Treat positive callable opportunity as fully included.

properties.name: options.roundCounts
properties.description: Round expected panel counts to integers.

returns.description: Matched WGS and panel spectra pairs for controlled validation.

namespaces.name: tools
namespaces.label: Utility tools
namespaces.summary: Small utility helpers shared by the public SDK.

functions.name: groupBy
functions.description: Groups array records by a selected key.

namespaces.name: machineLearning
namespaces.label: Machine learning
namespaces.summary: Preprocessing and cross-validation helpers used by browser workflows.

functions.name: kFoldCV
functions.description: Performs k-fold stratified cross-validation for multivariate linear or MLP regression models.

params.name: modelType
params.description: Regression model type ("MLR" for multivariate linear regression or "MLP" for multilayer perceptron).

returns.description: Object containing an array of trained regression models, an array of mean squared errors for each fold, and the average mean squared error across all folds.

functions.name: preprocessData
functions.description: Preprocesses mutational and exposure data for a given data source. Currently only supports data from MSigDB portal.

returns.description: Object containing input (Xs) and output (Ys) arrays for regression.

namespaces.name: signatureFitting
namespaces.label: Signature fitting
namespaces.summary: Known-signature exposure fitting and exposure plots.

functions.name: fitMutationalSpectraToSignatures
functions.description: Fits mutational spectra to known mutational signatures using non-negative least squares (NNLS).This function calculates the exposure of mutational signatures for each sample by fitting the observed mutational spectra to the reference mutational signatures. It then filters out signatures whose contribution is below a fraction of the total exposure, by setting their exposures to zero.You can choose to return exposures as absolute values (raw counts from NNLS) or relative values (sum to 1 for each sample). The filtering threshold is a fraction between 0 and 1.

params.name: mutationalSignatures
params.description: Reference mutational signatures. Each key is a signature name, and each value is an object of mutation types (e.g., {"C>A": weight, "C>G": weight}).

params.name: mutationalSpectra
params.description: Mutational spectra for each sample. Each key is a sample ID, and each value is an object of mutation types with their counts (e.g., {"C>A": count, "C>G": count}).

params.name: options
params.description: Configuration options for filtering and output.

properties.name: options.exposureThreshold
properties.description: Exclude signatures below this fraction of the total, by setting their exposures to zero. Must be between 0 and 1.

properties.name: options.exposureType
properties.description: Return exposures as absolute or relative.

properties.name: options.renormalize
properties.description: Whether to normalize exposures so that they sum to 1 after filtering.

returns.description: An object with sample IDs as keys. Each value is an object of signature exposures.

functions.name: plotDatasetMutationalSignaturesExposure
functions.description: Generates a heatmap visualizing the exposure of multiple samples to a set of mutational signatures within a dataset. The function provides options for displaying relative or absolute exposure values and for performing double hierarchical clustering to reorder the rows and columns of the heatmap. It also allows customization of the color scale used to represent exposure values.

params.name: exposureData
params.description: An object containing the exposure data for a set of samples. The structure of exposureData is expected to be: { sampleId1: { signatureName1: exposureValue1, signatureName2: exposureValue2, ..., rnorm: number }, sampleId2: { signatureName1: exposureValue3, signatureName2: exposureValue4, ..., rnorm: number }, ... } The outer keys (e.g., sampleId1, sampleId2) are sample identifiers (strings). The inner objects (e.g., { signatureName1: exposureValue1, ... }) represent the exposure values for a given sample. signatureName keys are strings representing the names of mutational signatures (e.g., "SBS1", "SBS5"), and exposureValue are non-negative numbers representing the contribution of that signature to the sample. rnorm is a number that will be removed from the data before plotting.

params.name: divID
params.description: The ID of the HTML div element where the heatmap will be rendered.

params.name: relative
params.description: A boolean indicating whether to display relative or absolute exposure values.true: The exposure values for each sample are normalized to sum to 1, representing the relative contribution of each signature.false: The raw exposure values are displayed.

params.name: datasetName
params.description: The name of the dataset being visualized. This is used as part of the plot title. Examples include:"PCAWG""TCGA"Any other string that appropriately identifies the dataset.

params.name: doubleCluster
params.description: A boolean indicating whether to perform double hierarchical clustering on the exposure data.true: The rows and columns of the heatmap are reordered based on the results of double clustering, which groups similar samples and signatures together.false: The rows and columns are displayed in the order they appear in the input exposureData.

params.name: colorscale
params.description: The color scale to use for the heatmap. Possible values are:"Custom": A predefined custom color scale designed for visualizing exposure data.Any valid Plotly color scale name (e.g., "Viridis", "Blues", "Hot", etc.).An array of arrays defining a custom color scale, where each inner array specifies a color stop with a value between 0 and 1 and a corresponding RGB color string (e.g., [["0.0", "rgb(49,54,149)"], ["1.0", "rgb(165,0,38)"]]).

returns.description: Returns the data object used by Plotly to generate the heatmap. This object contains the z values (exposure values), x values (signature names), y values (sample names), and other settings for the heatmap, including the color scale. The structure is: { z: [[exposureValue1, exposureValue2, ...], [exposureValue3, exposureValue4, ...], ...], x: [signatureName1, signatureName2, ...], y: [sampleId1, sampleId2, ...], type: "heatmap", colorscale: colorscale }

functions.name: plotPatientMutationalSignaturesExposure
functions.description: Generates a pie chart visualizing the exposure of a single sample to a set of mutational signatures. The function takes exposure data, which includes the relative contribution of each signature to the sample's mutational profile, and displays it in a pie chart format.

params.name: exposureData
params.description: An object containing exposure data for either a set of samples or a single sample. The structure of exposureData can be: { sampleId1: { signatureName1: exposureValue1, signatureName2: exposureValue2, ... }, sampleId2: { signatureName1: exposureValue3, signatureName2: exposureValue4, ... }, ... } or a single sample object: { signatureName1: exposureValue1, signatureName2: exposureValue2, ... }. The outer keys (e.g., sampleId1, sampleId2) are sample identifiers (strings). The inner objects (e.g., { signatureName1: exposureValue1, ... }) represent the exposure values for a given sample. signatureName keys are strings representing the names of mutational signatures (e.g., "SBS1", "SBS5"), and exposureValue are non-negative numbers representing the contribution of that signature to the sample. These values typically sum to 1 for each sample. The exposureData object can contain multiple samples, but only the data for the specified sample will be used for plotting. Each sample may also have an optional rnorm property which is a number.

params.name: divID
params.description: The ID of the HTML div element where the pie chart will be rendered.

params.name: sample
params.description: The ID of the sample for which to plot the mutational signature exposure. This should be one of the keys in the exposureData object (e.g., "sampleId1", "sampleId2").

returns.description: Returns the data object used by Plotly to generate the pie chart. This object contains the labels (signature names), values (exposure values), and other settings for the pie chart. The format is: { labels: [signatureName1, signatureName2, ...], values: [exposureValue1, exposureValue2, ...], name: "sample exposure values", textposition: "inside", hole: 0.4, hoverinfo: "name + value", type: "pie" }

namespaces.name: TCGA
namespaces.label: TCGA helpers
namespaces.summary: TCGA and GDC query helpers for projects, expression, MAF, and variant records.

functions.name: getMafInformationFromProjects

returns.description: Object containing the list of count ftpm and fpkm from each file organized by genes

functions.name: getTpmCountsByGenesOnProjects

params.name: genes

params.name: projects

returns.description: Object containing the list of count file ids and file descriptions organized by projects

functions.name: getVariantInformationFromMafFiles
functions.description: Obtain mutations and variant information given MAF file identifiers

params.name: res

properties.name: options.fileConcurrency

properties.name: options.includeVariantInformation

returns.description: Object containing the list of patient mutation information

functions.name: assertValid

params.name: label

returns.description: The original validation result when valid.

functions.name: getExpectedContexts
functions.description: Returns the canonical mutation-context order for supported profile matrices.

returns.description: Ordered context labels, or null for unsupported matrices.

functions.name: getMatrixContexts
functions.description: Returns all unique context or signature columns seen across matrix objects.

returns.description: Context or column names in first-seen order.

functions.name: getSBS96Contexts
functions.description: Returns the canonical SBS96 trinucleotide context labels.

returns.description: Ordered SBS96 context labels.

functions.name: normalizeMatrixObject
functions.description: Normalizes a nested matrix object to finite numeric values.

params.name: matrix
params.description: Matrix keyed by row and context.

returns.description: Numeric matrix with invalid values set to zero.

functions.name: rowsToMatrix
functions.description: Converts long-form rows into a matrix object keyed by sample or signature.

params.name: rows

returns.description: Matrix object.

functions.name: rowsToSampleSpectra

functions.name: rowsToSignatureMatrix

functions.name: validateExposureMatrix

returns.description: Validation result with validity, issues, warnings, samples, and signatures.

functions.name: validateMafRows
functions.description: Validates MAF-like rows before converting them into mutational spectra.

returns.description: Validation result with row count, required fields, and issues.

functions.name: validateSignatureMatrix
functions.description: Validates a reference or extracted signature matrix.

properties.name: options.strict
properties.description: Treat warnings as invalid when true.

returns.description: Validation result with validity, issues, warnings, signatures, and contexts.

functions.name: validateSpectra

properties.name: options.strict
properties.description: Treat warnings as invalid when true.

returns.description: Validation result with validity, issues, warnings, sample metrics, and contexts.

namespaces.name: qc
namespaces.label: QC and uncertainty
namespaces.summary: Mutation burden, reconstruction error, bootstrap, threshold, ambiguity, and fit evidence.

functions.name: bootstrapSignatureFit
functions.description: Bootstraps one spectrum to estimate exposure uncertainty and selection frequency.

returns.description: Bootstrap interval, selection-frequency, exposure, and reconstruction results.

functions.name: calculateFitResiduals
functions.description: Calculates observed, reconstructed, and residual spectra for fitted exposures.

returns.description: Residual object with contexts and per-sample metrics.

functions.name: calculateReconstructionError
functions.description: Summarizes reconstruction error metrics for fitted spectra.

returns.description: Per-sample cosine similarity, RMSE, and related residual metrics.

functions.name: fitSpectraWithNNLS
functions.description: Fits each sample spectrum to reference signatures using non-negative least squares.

properties.name: options.returnDetails
properties.description: Return exposures with solver metadata instead of the exposure matrix alone.

returns.description: Sample-by-signature exposure matrix, or a detailed result when returnDetails is true.

functions.name: normalizeExposures
functions.description: Normalizes an exposure matrix for downstream QC and reporting.

properties.name: options.relative
properties.description: Normalize each sample to sum to one.

properties.name: options.dropBelow
properties.description: Drop exposures at or below this value when zeroPolicy is "drop".

returns.description: Normalized exposure matrix.

functions.name: runThresholdSensitivity
functions.description: Sweeps exposure thresholds and records reconstruction and activity stability.

returns.description: Threshold grid and per-threshold fit metrics.

functions.name: selectSamplesByMutationBurden
functions.description: Selects sample-level burden records for teaching, reporting, or analysis.

properties.name: options.limit
properties.description: Maximum number of samples to return.

returns.description: Selected sample records from the burden summary.

functions.name: summarizeMissingContexts
functions.description: Reports missing and extra mutation contexts for each sample spectrum.

returns.description: Context coverage result with sample-level missing and extra contexts.

functions.name: summarizeMutationBurden
functions.description: Summarizes per-sample mutation burden and raises review cues for low-burden spectra.

properties.name: options.quantile
properties.description: Quantile used when threshold mode is "quantile".

returns.description: Sample-level burden metrics and overall summary counts.

params.description: Minimal options: contexts, exposureThreshold, lowBurdenThreshold, metadata, groupKey, reportFormat.

returns.description: Standard pipeline result frame.

functions.name: runDiscoveryWorkflow
functions.description: Runs cohort discovery with burden-aware gating, NMF rank selection, and reference matching.

params.name: input
params.description: Cohort spectra and optional reference signatures.

returns.description: Advisor, optional extraction, reference matches, and publication artifacts.

functions.name: runDiscoveryWorkflowLite
functions.description: Runs the beginner-facing discovery path with a fixed rank unless rank selection is requested.

params.name: input
params.description: Cohort spectra and optional reference signatures.

params.name: options
params.description: Minimal options: contexts, rank, ranks, runRankSelection, reportFormat.

returns.description: Standard discovery workflow result frame.

functions.name: runPanelWorkflow
functions.description: Runs a panel/WES-oriented workflow with opportunity normalization and review evidence tiers.

params.name: input
params.description: Panel or WES spectra, signatures, and optional callable opportunities.

returns.description: Fit result plus panel evidence calls and limitations.

functions.name: runPanelWorkflowLite
functions.description: Runs the beginner-facing panel/WES review path with stable defaults.

params.name: input
params.description: Restricted-assay spectra, signatures, and optional callable opportunities.

params.name: options
params.description: Minimal options: contexts, genomeBuild, callableOpportunities, referenceOpportunities, reportFormat.

returns.description: Standard panel workflow result frame.

functions.name: runSingleSampleFit
functions.description: Runs an opinionated single-sample signature refitting workflow.

params.name: options
params.description: Fitting, bootstrap, threshold, and reporting options.

returns.description: Fitted spectrum, fit-quality evidence, ambiguity, residuals, catalog check, and report artifacts.

functions.name: runSingleSampleFitLite
functions.description: Runs the beginner-facing single-sample refit path with a small option set.

params.name: options
params.description: Minimal options: contexts, sampleName, exposureThreshold, bootstrapIterations, genomeBuild, reportFormat.

returns.description: Standard pipeline result frame.

namespaces.name: workflows
namespaces.label: Workflow wrappers
namespaces.summary: High-level analysis wrappers for signature fitting and NMF workflows.

functions.name: analyzeMafFiles
functions.description: Converts MAF rows to spectra, then optionally fits known signatures and assembles QC/report outputs.

properties.name: options.groupBy
properties.description: MAF field used to group spectra.

properties.name: options.tcga
properties.description: Whether to apply TCGA-specific conversion behavior.

properties.name: options.fitting
properties.description: Options passed to analyzeSpectraWithSignatures when signatures are supplied.

returns.description: Spectra-only QC/report output or full signature-fitting workflow output.

functions.name: analyzeSpectraWithSignatures
functions.description: Fits sample spectra to known signatures and returns exposures, QC, provenance, and a report.

returns.description: Exposures, provenance, validation, QC, and report objects.

functions.name: createNMFAnalysis
functions.description: Runs NMF extraction and assembles validation, QC, optional reference comparison, and a report.

properties.name: input.referenceSignatures
properties.description: Optional reference signatures for post hoc matching.

properties.name: input.parameters
properties.description: Analysis parameters to include in the report.

returns.description: Validation, extraction, comparison, QC, and report objects.

functions.name: createSignatureFitAnalysis
functions.description: Assembles validation, QC, residuals, reconstruction metrics, and a report for fitted signatures.

properties.name: input.parameters
properties.description: Analysis parameters to include in the report.

returns.description: Validation, QC, and report objects.

functions.name: extractSignaturesFromSpectra
functions.description: Runs de novo NMF extraction and returns extraction, comparison, QC, provenance, and a report.

returns.description: Provenance, extraction, optional comparison, validation, QC, and report objects.

namespaces.name: quickstart
namespaces.label: Quickstart wrappers
namespaces.summary: Small-option entry points for MAF, cohort, panel, and report workflows.

functions.name: analyzeMafFilesLite
functions.description: Beginner-facing MAF analysis wrapper with a small option set.

params.name: options
params.description: Minimal options: groupBy, genome, offline, contextLookupTable, expectedContexts, reportFormat.

returns.description: MAF-to-spectra or signature-fitting workflow result.

namespaces.name: provenance
namespaces.label: Provenance
namespaces.summary: Reproducibility metadata for SDK version, source data, genome context, and runtime.

functions.name: createProvenance
functions.description: Creates a reproducibility record for an analysis result.

properties.name: options.parameters
properties.description: Parameters used to generate the result.

properties.name: options.sourceUrls
properties.description: Source URLs used by the analysis.

returns.description: Versioned SDK, runtime, source, and parameter metadata.

functions.name: withProvenance
functions.description: Wraps a result with a reproducibility record.

returns.description: Data plus provenance metadata.

namespaces.name: presentation
namespaces.label: Presentation helpers
namespaces.summary: Publication-context summaries and tooltip text for generated figures.

namespaces.name: runners
namespaces.label: Browser runtimes
namespaces.summary: Optional Pyodide and WebR execution helpers for compatible packages.

functions.name: checkWebRPackageAvailability
functions.description: Checks package availability in public or caller-supplied webR repositories.

returns.description: Package availability by name.

functions.name: createPyodideWorkerRunner
functions.description: Creates a reusable Pyodide Web Worker runner.

returns.description: Runner with run and terminate methods.

functions.name: createWebRRunner
functions.description: Creates a reusable webR runner.

returns.description: Runner with run and terminate methods.

functions.name: detectPyodideRuntime
functions.description: Reports whether the current JavaScript runtime can create a Pyodide worker.

returns.description: Runtime availability and missing browser capabilities.

functions.name: detectWebRRuntime
functions.description: Reports whether the current JavaScript runtime can create and communicate with a webR runtime.

returns.description: Runtime availability and missing browser capabilities.

functions.name: runPyodide
functions.description: Runs one Pyodide job in a temporary Web Worker.

params.name: payload
params.description: Python code, packages, files, and output collection options.

returns.description: Result, collected files, package metadata, and elapsed time.

functions.name: runPython
functions.description: Runs Python code in Pyodide with a small JavaScript-first API.The optional inputs object is serialized to MSIG_INPUT_JSON in the Python runtime, and string results are parsed as JSON by default so structured Python outputs are returned as ordinary JavaScript objects.

params.name: options
params.description: Python inputs, packages, files, output collection, and runner options.

properties.name: options.inputs
properties.description: JSON-serializable input object exposed as MSIG_INPUT_JSON.

returns.description: Result, collected files, package metadata, and elapsed time.

functions.name: runWebR
functions.description: Runs one webR job in a temporary runtime.

params.name: payload
params.description: R code, packages, files, and output collection options.

returns.description: Result, collected files, package metadata, and elapsed time.

namespaces.name: adapters
namespaces.label: External adapters
namespaces.summary: SigProfiler, deconstructSigs, sigminer, MuSiCal, COSMIC, and generic TSV handoffs.

functions.name: checkDeconstructSigsWebRAvailability
functions.description: Checks whether exact deconstructSigs execution can run through webR.

returns.description: Availability status with runtime and package details.

functions.name: checkSigminerWebRAvailability
functions.description: Checks whether exact sigminer execution can run through webR.

params.name: options
params.description: webR package repository and sigminer method options.

returns.description: Availability status with runtime and package details.

functions.name: createInteroperabilityBundle
functions.description: Creates a multi-tool interoperability bundle from the same spectra and catalog.

returns.description: Prepared handoff bundles for supported external tools.

functions.name: prepareDeconstructSigsInput
functions.description: Prepares input files and an R snippet for deconstructSigs handoff.

returns.description: Virtual files, manifest metadata, and an R command snippet.

functions.name: prepareMuSiCalRefitInput
functions.description: Prepares MuSiCal-compatible spectra and signature files for refitting.

returns.description: Virtual files and manifest metadata for refit workflows.

functions.name: prepareSigminerInput
functions.description: Prepares input files and an R snippet for sigminer known-signature fitting.

returns.description: Virtual files, manifest metadata, and an R command snippet.

functions.name: prepareSigProfilerAssignmentInput
functions.description: Prepares matrix-mode input files for SigProfilerAssignment.

returns.description: Virtual files and manifest metadata for a Pyodide run.

functions.name: prepareSigProfilerClustersInput
functions.description: Prepares input files and a Python snippet for SigProfilerClusters.

returns.description: Virtual files, manifest metadata, and a Python command snippet.

functions.name: prepareSigProfilerExtractorInput
functions.description: Prepares matrix-mode input files for SigProfilerExtractor handoff.

returns.description: Virtual files, manifest metadata, and a Python command snippet.

functions.name: prepareSigProfilerMatrixGeneratorInput
functions.description: Prepares variant files and a Python snippet for SigProfilerMatrixGenerator.

returns.description: Virtual files, manifest metadata, and a Python command snippet.

functions.name: prepareSigProfilerPlottingInput
functions.description: Prepares a matrix and Python snippet for sigProfilerPlotting.

properties.name: input.spectra
properties.description: Optional spectra to serialize as a SigProfiler matrix.

returns.description: Virtual files, manifest metadata, and a Python command snippet.

functions.name: prepareSigProfilerSimulatorInput
functions.description: Prepares input files and a Python snippet for SigProfilerSimulator.

properties.name: input.files
properties.description: Virtual input files placed under projectPath/input.

returns.description: Virtual files, manifest metadata, and a Python command snippet.

functions.name: runDeconstructSigsWebR
functions.description: Runs the deconstructSigs R package through webR when compatible package builds are available.

returns.description: Exact package result with parsed exposures.

functions.name: runMuSiCalRefit
functions.description: Runs MuSiCal refitting through Pyodide, or a browser-native sparse NNLS comparator.

returns.description: Refit result with exposures and provenance.

functions.name: runSigminerWebR
functions.description: Runs the sigminer R package through webR when compatible package builds are available.

params.name: options
params.description: Handoff, webR, package, and sigminer options.

returns.description: Exact package result with parsed exposures.

functions.name: runSigProfilerAssignment
functions.description: Runs SigProfilerAssignment in matrix mode through the Pyodide worker runner.

returns.description: Adapter result with collected files and parsed exposure table when detected.

functions.name: runSigProfilerExtractor
functions.description: Runs a browser-compatible SigProfilerExtractor handoff workflow.The default runtime uses browser-native NMF against the matrix prepared for SigProfilerExtractor. Exact package execution can still be requested with runtime="pyodide", but current SigProfilerExtractor wheels require torch, which is not installable in Pyodide.

returns.description: Adapter result with collected files and parsed output tables.

functions.name: runSparseNnlsRefit
functions.description: Runs a browser-native sparse NNLS refit comparator using MuSiCal-compatible inputs.This helper does not execute the MuSiCal Python package. It provides a small sparse refit comparator for browser review when a Pyodide-compatible MuSiCal wheel is not supplied.

returns.description: Sparse refit exposures and reconstruction metrics.
